=== PLAN ===
Build a p-code VM and p-code assembler (pasm) for the COR24 24-bit RISC ISA. The VM is a stack-based interpreter written in COR24 assembly. The pasm tool (also COR24 assembly) assembles human-readable .pasm source into bytecode for testing the VM. Pascal compiler is a separate project.

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-vm-scaffold [production]: Create pvm.s scaffold: COR24 entry point, VM state struct (pc, esp, csp, fp, ...
  [x] 002-vm-stack-ops [legacy]: Implement push/push_s/dup/drop/swap/over opcode handlers and sys PUTC (opcode 0x60 with id=1). Test by embedding hardcoded bytecode: push_s 72, sys 1, push_s 10, sys 1, halt — should print H-newline to UART.
  [x] 003-vm-arithmetic [legacy]: Implement add/sub/mul/div/mod/neg/and/or/xor/not/shl/shr and eq/ne/lt/le/gt/ge opcode handlers. Test with hardcoded bytecode: push 7, push 6, mul, sys PUTC should output ASCII '*' (42).
  [ ] 004-vm-control-flow [legacy]: Implement jmp/jz/jnz opcode handlers. Test with hardcoded countdown loop: push 5, loop: dup, jz done, push_s 1, sub, jmp loop, done: halt. <-- YOU ARE HERE
  [ ] 005-vm-frames [legacy]: Implement call/ret/enter/leave and loadl/storel/loada/storea opcode handlers. Build frame management: push/pop activation records on call stack, argument passing from eval stack to frame. Test with hardcoded bytecode for a factorial function.
  [ ] 006-vm-memory [legacy]: Implement loadg/storeg/addrg/addrl and indirect load/store/loadb/storeb. Implement globals segment. Test with hardcoded bytecode: global counter, store/load/print.
  [ ] 007-vm-nonlocal [legacy]: Implement loadn/storen for static link chain traversal (needed for Pascal nested procedures). Test with hardcoded 2-level nested procedure accessing outer local.
  [ ] 008-vm-syscalls [legacy]: Implement remaining sys calls: HALT(0), GETC(2), LED(3), ALLOC(4), FREE(5). Bump allocator for heap. Test: LED toggle, UART echo, heap alloc/write/read.
  [ ] 009-vm-traps [legacy]: Implement trap handling for all trap codes (div-zero, stack overflow/underflow, invalid opcode, invalid address, nil pointer). Print diagnostic to UART on trap. Test each trap condition.
  [ ] 010-pasm-lexer [legacy]: Create pasm.s — p-code assembler in COR24 assembly. Start with lexer: read pasm source from UART, tokenize into token types (mnemonic, number, label, directive, newline, comment, eof). Use a token buffer in memory. Test by feeding simple pasm source and dumping token stream.
  [ ] 011-pasm-parser [legacy]: Implement pasm parser: two-pass assembly. Pass 1: collect labels and procedure addresses, count globals/data sizes. Pass 2: emit bytecode. Handle .const/.global/.data/.proc/.end directives. Test by assembling a minimal program (push 42, halt) and verifying bytecode bytes.
  [ ] 012-pasm-integration [legacy]: Connect pasm output to VM input. Assemble pasm source into bytecode buffer, then execute. Test with hello.pasm (full puts procedure, data string, main calling puts). First end-to-end test.
  [ ] 013-test-suite [legacy]: Create full test suite: tests/t01-arith.pasm through t10-traps.pasm. Create demo.sh test harness with golden output comparison (following tf24a pattern). Create examples/hello.pasm, examples/blink.pasm, examples/echo.pasm.
  [ ] 014-hardening [legacy]: Fix bugs found by test suite. Add stack depth checking. Validate memory bounds. Clean up diagnostic output. Ensure all tests pass. Final documentation update.

=== CURRENT STEP: 004-vm-control-flow [legacy] ===
Status: pending
Description: Implement jmp/jz/jnz opcode handlers. Test with hardcoded countdown loop: push 5, loop: dup, jz done, push_s 1, sub, jmp loop, done: halt.

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

1→# pv24a — Design Document
     2→
     3→## 1. VM State
     4→
     5→The p-code VM maintains the following state in a memory struct:
     6→
     7→```
     8→vm_state:
     9→    .word 0     ; pc   — p-code instruction pointer
    10→    .word 0     ; esp  — eval stack pointer (top of eval stack)
    11→    .word 0     ; csp  — call stack pointer (top of call stack)
    12→    .word 0     ; fp   — current frame pointer (call stack)
    13→    .word 0     ; gp   — globals base address
    14→    .word 0     ; hp   — heap pointer (next free)
    15→    .word 0     ; code — base address of p-code segment
    16→    .word 0     ; status — 0=running, 1=halted, 2=trapped
    17→    .word 0     ; trap_code — last trap reason
    18→```
    19→
    20→## 2. P-Code Instruction Set
    21→
    22→### 2.1 Stack / Constants
    23→
    24→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    25→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    26→| `push` imm24 | 4 bytes | ( -- n ) | Push 24-bit immediate |
    27→| `push_s` imm8 | 2 bytes | ( -- n ) | Push sign-extended 8-bit immediate |
    28→| `dup` | 1 byte | ( a -- a a ) | Duplicate top |
    29→| `drop` | 1 byte | ( a -- ) | Discard top |
    30→| `swap` | 1 byte | ( a b -- b a ) | Swap top two |
    31→| `over` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a b a ) | Copy second to top |
    32→
    33→### 2.2 Arithmetic / Logic
    34→
    35→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    36→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    37→| `add` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a+b ) | Add |
    38→| `sub` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a-b ) | Subtract |
    39→| `mul` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a*b ) | Multiply |
    40→| `div` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a/b ) | Divide (trap on b=0) |
    41→| `mod` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a%b ) | Modulo (trap on b=0) |
    42→| `neg` | 1 byte | ( a -- -a ) | Negate |
    43→| `and` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a&b ) | Bitwise AND |
    44→| `or` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a\|b ) | Bitwise OR |
    45→| `xor` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a^b ) | Bitwise XOR |
    46→| `not` | 1 byte | ( a -- ~a ) | Bitwise NOT |
    47→| `shl` | 1 byte | ( a n -- a<<n ) | Shift left |
    48→| `shr` | 1 byte | ( a n -- a>>n ) | Arithmetic shift right |
    49→
    50→### 2.3 Comparison
    51→
    52→All comparisons push 1 (true) or 0 (false):
    53→
    54→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    55→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    56→| `eq` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Equal |
    57→| `ne` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Not equal |
    58→| `lt` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Less than (signed) |
    59→| `le` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Less or equal (signed) |
    60→| `gt` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Greater than (signed) |
    61→| `ge` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Greater or equal (signed) |
    62→
    63→### 2.4 Control Flow
    64→
    65→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    66→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    67→| `jmp` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( -- ) | Unconditional jump |
    68→| `jz` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( flag -- ) | Jump if zero |
    69→| `jnz` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( flag -- ) | Jump if nonzero |
    70→| `call` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( args... -- ) | Call procedure |
    71→| `ret` nargs8 | 2 bytes | ( [retval] -- [retval] ) | Return, clean nargs |
    72→| `halt` | 1 byte | ( -- ) | Stop VM |
    73→| `trap` code8 | 2 bytes | ( -- ) | Trigger trap |
    74→
    75→### 2.5 Local / Global / Nonlocal Access
    76→
    77→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    78→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    79→| `enter` nlocals8 | 2 bytes | ( -- ) | Set up frame, reserve N local slots |
    80→| `leave` | 1 byte | ( -- ) | Tear down frame |
    81→| `loadl` off8 | 2 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load local at offset |
    82→| `storel` off8 | 2 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store local at offset |
    83→| `loadg` off24 | 4 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load global at offset |
    84→| `storeg` off24 | 4 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store global at offset |
    85→| `addrl` off8 | 2 bytes | ( -- addr ) | Push address of local |
    86→| `addrg` off24 | 4 bytes | ( -- addr ) | Push address of global |
    87→| `loadn` depth8 off8 | 3 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load nonlocal via static link chain |
    88→| `storen` depth8 off8 | 3 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store nonlocal via static link chain |
    89→
    90→### 2.6 Indirect Memory Access
    91→
    92→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    93→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    94→| `load` | 1 byte | ( addr -- val ) | Load word from address |
    95→| `store` | 1 byte | ( val addr -- ) | Store word to address |
    96→| `loadb` | 1 byte | ( addr -- byte ) | Load byte (zero-extended) |
    97→| `storeb` | 1 byte | ( byte addr -- ) | Store byte |
    98→
    99→### 2.7 System / I/O
   100→
   101→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
   102→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
   103→| `sys` id8 | 2 bytes | (varies) | System call |
   104→
   105→System call IDs:
   106→
   107→| ID | Name | Stack Effect | Description |
   108→|----|------|-------------|-------------|
   109→| 0 | HALT | ( -- ) | Stop execution |
   110→| 1 | PUTC | ( char -- ) | Write byte to UART |
   111→| 2 | GETC | ( -- char ) | Read byte from UART (blocking) |
   112→| 3 | LED | ( state -- ) | Write LED state |
   113→| 4 | ALLOC | ( size -- ptr ) | Bump-allocate heap block |
   114→| 5 | FREE | ( ptr -- ) | Free heap block (no-op in bump mode) |
   115→
   116→### 2.8 Argument Access
   117→
   118→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
   119→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
   120→| `loada` idx8 | 2 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load argument by index |
   121→| `storea` idx8 | 2 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store argument by index |
   122→
   123→## 3. Call Frame Layout
   124→
   125→When `call` executes, a new frame is pushed onto the call stack:
   126→
   127→```
   128→         ┌──────────────────┐  ← new csp (after enter)
   129→         │ local[N-1]       │  offset = +(N-1)*3 from fp
   130→         │ ...              │
   131→         │ local[0]         │  offset = 0 from fp
   132→fp ───→  ├──────────────────┤
   133→         │ procedure ID     │  offset = -3
   134→         │ static link      │  offset = -6  (lexically enclosing frame)
   135→         │ dynamic link     │  offset = -9  (caller's fp)
   136→         │ return PC        │  offset = -12
   137→         ├──────────────────┤
   138→         │ arg[N-1]         │  offset = -15  (from fp)
   139→         │ ...              │
   140→         │ arg[0]           │  offset = -15 - (N-1)*3
   141→         └──────────────────┘
   142→```
   143→
   144→- **Caller** pushes arguments onto eval stack, then `call addr`
   145→- **call** instruction: pops args from eval stack into call stack frame header,
   146→  pushes return PC, dynamic link, static link, proc ID
   147→- **enter N**: advances csp by N*3 to reserve local slots, sets fp
   148→- **leave**: restores fp to dynamic link, restores csp
   149→- **ret N**: pushes return value (if any) to eval stack, pops frame, cleans N args
   150→
   151→## 4. Calling Convention
   152→
   153→Simple and explicit:
   154→
   155→1. Caller pushes arguments left-to-right onto eval stack
   156→2. Caller executes `call <proc_addr>`
   157→3. Callee executes `enter <nlocals>` (reserves local slots)
   158→4. Callee accesses args via `loada 0`, `loada 1`, etc.
   159→5. Callee accesses locals via `loadl 0`, `loadl 1`, etc.
   160→6. For functions: callee pushes return value onto eval stack
   161→7. Callee executes `ret <nargs>` (cleans args from frame)
   162→8. Caller finds return value (if any) on eval stack
   163→
   164→## 5. P-Code Assembler (pasm) Syntax
   165→
   166→### 5.0 File Extensions
   167→
   168→- `.spc` — p-code assembler source files (input to pasm)
   169→- `.p24` — assembled p-code bytecode files (output of pasm, input to VM)
   170→
   171→### 5.1 Source Format
   172→
   173→```
   174→; Comment (semicolon to end of line)
   175→
   176→.const UART_DATA  -65280      ; named constant
   177→.const UART_STATUS -65279
   178→.const LED_PORT   -65024
   179→
   180→.global counter 1             ; 1 word of global storage
   181→.global buffer 32             ; 32 words
   182→
   183→.data msg 72, 101, 108, 108, 111, 0   ; "Hello\0" as bytes
   184→
   185→.proc main 0                 ; procedure, 0 locals
   186→    push msg                  ; push address of msg
   187→    call puts
   188→    halt
   189→.end
   190→
   191→.proc puts 1                 ; 1 local (loop pointer)
   192→    loada 0                   ; arg0 = string address
   193→    storel 0                  ; local0 = current ptr
   194→loop:
   195→    loadl 0
   196→    loadb                     ; load byte at ptr
   197→    dup
   198→    jz done                   ; if zero, done
   199→    sys 1                     ; PUTC
   200→    loadl 0
   201→    push 1
   202→    add
   203→    storel 0                  ; ptr++
   204→    jmp loop
   205→done:
   206→    drop                      ; drop the zero
   207→    ret 1                     ; return, clean 1 arg
   208→.end
   209→```
   210→
   211→### 5.2 Directives
   212→
   213→| Directive | Syntax | Description |
   214→|-----------|--------|-------------|
   215→| `.const` | `.const NAME value` | Define named constant |
   216→| `.global` | `.global NAME nwords` | Reserve global storage |
   217→| `.data` | `.data NAME byte, byte, ...` | Define byte data (string/array) |
   218→| `.proc` | `.proc NAME nlocals` | Begin procedure (nlocals = local slot count) |
   219→| `.end` | `.end` | End procedure |
   220→
   221→### 5.3 Labels
   222→
   223→Labels appear on their own line, ending with `:`:
   224→
   225→```
   226→loop:
   227→    ; instructions...
   228→    jmp loop
   229→```
   230→
   231→Labels are local to the enclosing `.proc` / `.end` block.
   232→
   233→### 5.4 Integer Literals
   234→
   235→- Decimal: `42`, `-1`, `0`
   236→- Hex: `0xFF` (if supported) or decimal equivalents
   237→- Named constants: `UART_DATA` (substituted by assembler)
   238→
   239→### 5.5 Instructions
   240→
   241→One instruction per line. Operand follows mnemonic separated by whitespace:
   242→
   243→```
   244→push 42
   245→loadl 0
   246→jz done
   247→call puts
   248→sys 1
   249→ret 0
   250→```
   251→
   252→## 6. Opcode Encoding Table
   253→
   254→Opcodes are assigned sequential byte values:
   255→
   256→| Byte | Mnemonic | Operand |
   257→|------|----------|---------|
   258→| 0x00 | halt | — |
   259→| 0x01 | push | imm24 |
   260→| 0x02 | push_s | imm8 |
   261→| 0x03 | dup | — |
   262→| 0x04 | drop | — |
   263→| 0x05 | swap | — |
   264→| 0x06 | over | — |
   265→| 0x10 | add | — |
   266→| 0x11 | sub | — |
   267→| 0x12 | mul | — |
   268→| 0x13 | div | — |
   269→| 0x14 | mod | — |
   270→| 0x15 | neg | — |
   271→| 0x16 | and | — |
   272→| 0x17 | or | — |
   273→| 0x18 | xor | — |
   274→| 0x19 | not | — |
   275→| 0x1A | shl | — |
   276→| 0x1B | shr | — |
   277→| 0x20 | eq | — |
   278→| 0x21 | ne | — |
   279→| 0x22 | lt | — |
   280→| 0x23 | le | — |
   281→| 0x24 | gt | — |
   282→| 0x25 | ge | — |
   283→| 0x30 | jmp | addr24 |
   284→| 0x31 | jz | addr24 |
   285→| 0x32 | jnz | addr24 |
   286→| 0x33 | call | addr24 |
   287→| 0x34 | ret | nargs8 |
   288→| 0x35 | trap | code8 |
   289→| 0x40 | enter | nlocals8 |
   290→| 0x41 | leave | — |
   291→| 0x42 | loadl | off8 |
   292→| 0x43 | storel | off8 |
   293→| 0x44 | loadg | off24 |
   294→| 0x45 | storeg | off24 |
   295→| 0x46 | addrl | off8 |
   296→| 0x47 | addrg | off24 |
   297→| 0x48 | loada | idx8 |
   298→| 0x49 | storea | idx8 |
   299→| 0x4A | loadn | depth8 off8 |
   300→| 0x4B | storen | depth8 off8 |
   301→| 0x50 | load | — |
   302→| 0x51 | store | — |
   303→| 0x52 | loadb | — |
   304→| 0x53 | storeb | — |
   305→| 0x60 | sys | id8 |
   306→
   307→Reserved ranges 0x70–0xFF for future opcodes (heap, debug, extended ops).
   308→
   309→## 7. Trap Codes
   310→
   311→| Code | Name | Trigger |
   312→|------|------|---------|
   313→| 0 | USER_TRAP | `trap 0` instruction |
   314→| 1 | DIV_ZERO | Division/modulo by zero |
   315→| 2 | STACK_OVERFLOW | Eval or call stack exceeds limit |
   316→| 3 | STACK_UNDERFLOW | Eval stack pop when empty |
   317→| 4 | INVALID_OPCODE | Unknown opcode byte |
   318→| 5 | INVALID_ADDRESS | Memory access out of bounds |
   319→| 6 | NIL_POINTER | Dereference of address 0 |
   320→| 7 | BOUNDS_CHECK | Array index out of bounds |
   321→
   322→On trap: VM sets status=2, trap_code=N, halts. Diagnostic message to UART.
   323→
   324→## 8. Memory Segment Layout
   325→
   326→At VM initialization:
   327→
   328→1. **Code segment** starts at a fixed base address (after VM code)
   329→2. **Constants/data** follows code (emitted by pasm)
   330→3. **Globals** follow data (allocated per `.global` directives)
   331→4. **Call stack** starts after globals, grows upward
   332→5. **Eval stack** starts after call stack region, grows upward
   333→6. **Heap** starts after eval stack region, grows upward
   334→7. Segment base addresses stored in VM state struct
   335→
   336→Sizes are configurable at VM init time. Default allocations:
   337→- Call stack: 4 KB
   338→- Eval stack: 2 KB
   339→- Heap: remaining SRAM up to ~900 KB
   340→
   341→## 9. Implementation Strategy
   342→
   343→### Phase 1: Minimal VM
   344→- Opcode dispatch loop
   345→- push, dup, drop, add, sub, halt
   346→- UART output via sys PUTC
   347→- Hardcoded test bytecode (no pasm yet)
   348→
   349→### Phase 2: Control Flow + Procedures
   350→- jmp, jz, jnz
   351→- call, ret, enter, leave
   352→- loadl, storel, loada, storea
   353→- Frame management
   354→
   355→### Phase 3: Full Instruction Set
   356→- All arithmetic/logic/comparison opcodes
   357→- Globals (loadg, storeg)
   358→- Indirect memory (load, store, loadb, storeb)
   359→- Nonlocal access (loadn, storen)
   360→- All sys calls
   361→
   362→### Phase 4: P-Code Assembler
   363→- Lexer for pasm syntax (UART input)
   364→- Two-pass assembly (collect labels, emit bytes)
   365→- Directive handling (.const, .global, .data, .proc/.end)
   366→- Output bytecode to memory buffer
   367→
   368→### Phase 5: Integration & Test Suite
   369→- pasm assembles test programs
   370→- VM executes assembled bytecode
   371→- Regression test harness (demo.sh)
   372→- Full test coverage per staged plan
   373→
   374→## 10. COR24 Assembly Patterns
   375→
   376→Reference patterns from tf24a for common idioms:
   377→
   378→### Dispatch Table
   379→```asm
   380→; Index into jump table by opcode byte
   381→; r0 = opcode
   382→la r2, dispatch_table
   383→; multiply r0 by 3 (entry size = .word = 3 bytes)
   384→; ... load target address, jmp
   385→```
   386→
   387→### UART Output
   388→```asm
   389→; r0 = character to send

# pv24a — Design Document

## 1. VM State

The p-code VM maintains the following state in a memory struct:

```
vm_state:
    .word 0     ; pc   — p-code instruction pointer
    .word 0     ; esp  — eval stack pointer (top of eval stack)
    .word 0     ; csp  — call stack pointer (top of call stack)
    .word 0     ; fp   — current frame pointer (call stack)
    .word 0     ; gp   — globals base address
    .word 0     ; hp   — heap pointer (next free)
    .word 0     ; code — base address of p-code segment
    .word 0     ; status — 0=running, 1=halted, 2=trapped
    .word 0     ; trap_code — last trap reason
```

## 2. P-Code Instruction Set

### 2.1 Stack / Constants

| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `push` imm24 | 4 bytes | ( -- n ) | Push 24-bit immediate |
| `push_s` imm8 | 2 bytes | ( -- n ) | Push sign-extended 8-bit immediate |
| `dup` | 1 byte | ( a -- a a ) | Duplicate top |
| `drop` | 1 byte | ( a -- ) | Discard top |
| `swap` | 1 byte | ( a b -- b a ) | Swap top two |
| `over` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a b a ) | Copy second to top |

### 2.2 Arithmetic / Logic

| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `add` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a+b ) | Add |
| `sub` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a-b ) | Subtract |
| `mul` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a*b ) | Multiply |
| `div` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a/b ) | Divide (trap on b=0) |
| `mod` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a%b ) | Modulo (trap on b=0) |
| `neg` | 1 byte | ( a -- -a ) | Negate |
| `and` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a&b ) | Bitwise AND |
| `or` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a\|b ) | Bitwise OR |
| `xor` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a^b ) | Bitwise XOR |
| `not` | 1 byte | ( a -- ~a ) | Bitwise NOT |
| `shl` | 1 byte | ( a n -- a<<n ) | Shift left |
| `shr` | 1 byte | ( a n -- a>>n ) | Arithmetic shift right |

### 2.3 Comparison

All comparisons push 1 (true) or 0 (false):

| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `eq` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Equal |
| `ne` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Not equal |
| `lt` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Less than (signed) |
| `le` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Less or equal (signed) |
| `gt` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Greater than (signed) |
| `ge` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Greater or equal (signed) |

### 2.4 Control Flow

| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `jmp` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( -- ) | Unconditional jump |
| `jz` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( flag -- ) | Jump if zero |
| `jnz` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( flag -- ) | Jump if nonzero |
| `call` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( args... -- ) | Call procedure |
| `ret` nargs8 | 2 bytes | ( [retval] -- [retval] ) | Return, clean nargs |
| `halt` | 1 byte | ( -- ) | Stop VM |
| `trap` code8 | 2 bytes | ( -- ) | Trigger trap |

### 2.5 Local / Global / Nonlocal Access

| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `enter` nlocals8 | 2 bytes | ( -- ) | Set up frame, reserve N local slots |
| `leave` | 1 byte | ( -- ) | Tear down frame |
| `loadl` off8 | 2 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load local at offset |
| `storel` off8 | 2 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store local at offset |
| `loadg` off24 | 4 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load global at offset |
| `storeg` off24 | 4 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store global at offset |
| `addrl` off8 | 2 bytes | ( -- addr ) | Push address of local |
| `addrg` off24 | 4 bytes | ( -- addr ) | Push address of global |
| `loadn` depth8 off8 | 3 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load nonlocal via static link chain |
| `storen` depth8 off8 | 3 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store nonlocal via static link chain |

### 2.6 Indirect Memory Access

| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `load` | 1 byte | ( addr -- val ) | Load word from address |
| `store` | 1 byte | ( val addr -- ) | Store word to address |
| `loadb` | 1 byte | ( addr -- byte ) | Load byte (zero-extended) |
| `storeb` | 1 byte | ( byte addr -- ) | Store byte |

### 2.7 System / I/O

| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `sys` id8 | 2 bytes | (varies) | System call |

System call IDs:

| ID | Name | Stack Effect | Description |
|----|------|-------------|-------------|
| 0 | HALT | ( -- ) | Stop execution |
| 1 | PUTC | ( char -- ) | Write byte to UART |
| 2 | GETC | ( -- char ) | Read byte from UART (blocking) |
| 3 | LED | ( state -- ) | Write LED state |
| 4 | ALLOC | ( size -- ptr ) | Bump-allocate heap block |
| 5 | FREE | ( ptr -- ) | Free heap block (no-op in bump mode) |

### 2.8 Argument Access

| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `loada` idx8 | 2 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load argument by index |
| `storea` idx8 | 2 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store argument by index |

## 3. Call Frame Layout

When `call` executes, a new frame is pushed onto the call stack:

```
         ┌──────────────────┐  ← new csp (after enter)
         │ local[N-1]       │  offset = +(N-1)*3 from fp
         │ ...              │
         │ local[0]         │  offset = 0 from fp
fp ───→  ├──────────────────┤
         │ procedure ID     │  offset = -3
         │ static link      │  offset = -6  (lexically enclosing frame)
         │ dynamic link     │  offset = -9  (caller's fp)
         │ return PC        │  offset = -12
         ├──────────────────┤
         │ arg[N-1]         │  offset = -15  (from fp)
         │ ...              │
         │ arg[0]           │  offset = -15 - (N-1)*3
         └──────────────────┘
```

- **Caller** pushes arguments onto eval stack, then `call addr`
- **call** instruction: pops args from eval stack into call stack frame header,
  pushes return PC, dynamic link, static link, proc ID
- **enter N**: advances csp by N*3 to reserve local slots, sets fp
- **leave**: restores fp to dynamic link, restores csp
- **ret N**: pushes return value (if any) to eval stack, pops frame, cleans N args

## 4. Calling Convention

Simple and explicit:

1. Caller pushes arguments left-to-right onto eval stack
2. Caller executes `call <proc_addr>`
3. Callee executes `enter <nlocals>` (reserves local slots)
4. Callee accesses args via `loada 0`, `loada 1`, etc.
5. Callee accesses locals via `loadl 0`, `loadl 1`, etc.
6. For functions: callee pushes return value onto eval stack
7. Callee executes `ret <nargs>` (cleans args from frame)
8. Caller finds return value (if any) on eval stack

## 5. P-Code Assembler (pasm) Syntax

### 5.0 File Extensions

- `.spc` — p-code assembler source files (input to pasm)
- `.p24` — assembled p-code bytecode files (output of pasm, input to VM)

### 5.1 Source Format

```
; Comment (semicolon to end of line)

.const UART_DATA  -65280      ; named constant
.const UART_STATUS -65279
.const LED_PORT   -65024

.global counter 1             ; 1 word of global storage
.global buffer 32             ; 32 words

.data msg 72, 101, 108, 108, 111, 0   ; "Hello\0" as bytes

.proc main 0                 ; procedure, 0 locals
    push msg                  ; push address of msg
    call puts
    halt
.end

.proc puts 1                 ; 1 local (loop pointer)
    loada 0                   ; arg0 = string address
    storel 0                  ; local0 = current ptr
loop:
    loadl 0
    loadb                     ; load byte at ptr
    dup
    jz done                   ; if zero, done
    sys 1                     ; PUTC
    loadl 0
    push 1
    add
    storel 0                  ; ptr++
    jmp loop
done:
    drop                      ; drop the zero
    ret 1                     ; return, clean 1 arg
.end
```

### 5.2 Directives

| Directive | Syntax | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `.const` | `.const NAME value` | Define named constant |
| `.global` | `.global NAME nwords` | Reserve global storage |
| `.data` | `.data NAME byte, byte, ...` | Define byte data (string/array) |
| `.proc` | `.proc NAME nlocals` | Begin procedure (nlocals = local slot count) |
| `.end` | `.end` | End procedure |

### 5.3 Labels

Labels appear on their own line, ending with `:`:

```
loop:
    ; instructions...
    jmp loop
```

Labels are local to the enclosing `.proc` / `.end` block.

### 5.4 Integer Literals

- Decimal: `42`, `-1`, `0`
- Hex: `0xFF` (if supported) or decimal equivalents
- Named constants: `UART_DATA` (substituted by assembler)

### 5.5 Instructions

One instruction per line. Operand follows mnemonic separated by whitespace:

```
push 42
loadl 0
jz done
call puts
sys 1
ret 0
```

## 6. Opcode Encoding Table

Opcodes are assigned sequential byte values:

| Byte | Mnemonic | Operand |
|------|----------|---------|
| 0x00 | halt | — |
| 0x01 | push | imm24 |
| 0x02 | push_s | imm8 |
| 0x03 | dup | — |
| 0x04 | drop | — |
| 0x05 | swap | — |
| 0x06 | over | — |
| 0x10 | add | — |
| 0x11 | sub | — |
| 0x12 | mul | — |
| 0x13 | div | — |
| 0x14 | mod | — |
| 0x15 | neg | — |
| 0x16 | and | — |
| 0x17 | or | — |
| 0x18 | xor | — |
| 0x19 | not | — |
| 0x1A | shl | — |
| 0x1B | shr | — |
| 0x20 | eq | — |
| 0x21 | ne | — |
| 0x22 | lt | — |
| 0x23 | le | — |
| 0x24 | gt | — |
| 0x25 | ge | — |
| 0x30 | jmp | addr24 |
| 0x31 | jz | addr24 |
| 0x32 | jnz | addr24 |
| 0x33 | call | addr24 |
| 0x34 | ret | nargs8 |
| 0x35 | trap | code8 |
| 0x40 | enter | nlocals8 |
| 0x41 | leave | — |
| 0x42 | loadl | off8 |
| 0x43 | storel | off8 |
| 0x44 | loadg | off24 |
| 0x45 | storeg | off24 |
| 0x46 | addrl | off8 |
| 0x47 | addrg | off24 |
| 0x48 | loada | idx8 |
| 0x49 | storea | idx8 |
| 0x4A | loadn | depth8 off8 |
| 0x4B | storen | depth8 off8 |
| 0x50 | load | — |
| 0x51 | store | — |
| 0x52 | loadb | — |
| 0x53 | storeb | — |
| 0x60 | sys | id8 |

Reserved ranges 0x70–0xFF for future opcodes (heap, debug, extended ops).

## 7. Trap Codes

| Code | Name | Trigger |
|------|------|---------|
| 0 | USER_TRAP | `trap 0` instruction |
| 1 | DIV_ZERO | Division/modulo by zero |
| 2 | STACK_OVERFLOW | Eval or call stack exceeds limit |
| 3 | STACK_UNDERFLOW | Eval stack pop when empty |
| 4 | INVALID_OPCODE | Unknown opcode byte |
| 5 | INVALID_ADDRESS | Memory access out of bounds |
| 6 | NIL_POINTER | Dereference of address 0 |
| 7 | BOUNDS_CHECK | Array index out of bounds |

On trap: VM sets status=2, trap_code=N, halts. Diagnostic message to UART.

## 8. Memory Segment Layout

At VM initialization:

1. **Code segment** starts at a fixed base address (after VM code)
2. **Constants/data** follows code (emitted by pasm)
3. **Globals** follow data (allocated per `.global` directives)
4. **Call stack** starts after globals, grows upward
5. **Eval stack** starts after call stack region, grows upward
6. **Heap** starts after eval stack region, grows upward
7. Segment base addresses stored in VM state struct

Sizes are configurable at VM init time. Default allocations:
- Call stack: 4 KB
- Eval stack: 2 KB
- Heap: remaining SRAM up to ~900 KB

## 9. Implementation Strategy

### Phase 1: Minimal VM
- Opcode dispatch loop
- push, dup, drop, add, sub, halt
- UART output via sys PUTC
- Hardcoded test bytecode (no pasm yet)

### Phase 2: Control Flow + Procedures
- jmp, jz, jnz
- call, ret, enter, leave
- loadl, storel, loada, storea
- Frame management

### Phase 3: Full Instruction Set
- All arithmetic/logic/comparison opcodes
- Globals (loadg, storeg)
- Indirect memory (load, store, loadb, storeb)
- Nonlocal access (loadn, storen)
- All sys calls

### Phase 4: P-Code Assembler
- Lexer for pasm syntax (UART input)
- Two-pass assembly (collect labels, emit bytes)
- Directive handling (.const, .global, .data, .proc/.end)
- Output bytecode to memory buffer

### Phase 5: Integration & Test Suite
- pasm assembles test programs
- VM executes assembled bytecode
- Regression test harness (demo.sh)
- Full test coverage per staged plan

## 10. COR24 Assembly Patterns

Reference patterns from tf24a for common idioms:

### Dispatch Table
```asm
; Index into jump table by opcode byte
; r0 = opcode
la r2, dispatch_table
; multiply r0 by 3 (entry size = .word = 3 bytes)
; ... load target address, jmp
```

### UART Output
```asm
; r0 = character to send
la r2, -65280       ; UART base
tx_wait:
    lb r0, 1(r2)   ; status (sign-extended)
    cls r0, z       ; C = (status < 0) = TX busy
    brt tx_wait
    sb r0, 0(r2)   ; write byte
```

### Memory Struct Access
```asm
; Load vm_state.pc into r0
la fp, vm_state
lw r0, 0(fp)       ; pc at offset 0
```

1→; pvm.s — P-Code Virtual Machine for COR24
     2→;
     3→; Register allocation:
     4→;   r0 = W (work/scratch, opcode dispatch)
     5→;   r1 = scratch / return address for jal calls
     6→;   r2 = scratch
     7→;   sp = COR24 hardware stack (EBR, used sparingly)
     8→;   fp = memory base for indexed loads/stores
     9→;
    10→; VM state lives in memory struct (vm_state) because COR24 has only 3 GPRs.
    11→;
    12→; UART: data at -65280 (0xFF0100), status at -65279 (0xFF0101)
    13→;   TX busy = status bit 7 (sign bit via lb sign-extend)
    14→;   RX ready = status bit 0
    15→; LED: port at -65024 (0xFF0200)
    16→;
    17→; COR24 ISA notes:
    18→;   lbu = load byte zero-extend, lb = load byte sign-extend
    19→;   ceq ra, rb sets C if ra == rb; cls ra, rb sets C if ra < rb (signed)
    20→;   clu ra, rb sets C if ra < rb (unsigned)
    21→;   brt/brf = branch if C true/false; bra = branch always
    22→;   jal r1, (r2) = r1 = PC+1, PC = r2 (call convention)
    23→;   jmp (r1) = return from jal call
    24→;   Valid load/store base registers: r0, r1, r2, fp (NOT sp)
    25→;   Can push/pop: r0, r1, r2, fp
    26→
    27→; ============================================================
    28→; Entry point
    29→; ============================================================
    30→_start:
    31→    ; Initialize VM state struct (fp = &vm_state)
    32→    la r0, vm_state
    33→    push r0
    34→    pop fp
    35→
    36→    ; pc = 0
    37→    lc r0, 0
    38→    sw r0, 0(fp)
    39→
    40→    ; esp = eval_stack base
    41→    la r0, eval_stack
    42→    sw r0, 3(fp)
    43→
    44→    ; csp = call_stack base
    45→    la r0, call_stack

    47→
    48→    ; fp_vm = 0 (no frame yet)

    59→

    61→    la r0, code_seg

   247→    sw r2, 3(fp)

   249→    jmp (r0)

   251→; 0x02 — push_s imm8: fetch 1-byte sign-extended operand, push onto eval stack
   252→op_push_s:

   254→    lw r0, 18(fp)
   255→    lw r2, 0(fp)
   256→    add r0, r2

   258→    lb r2, 0(r0)

   260→    push r2

   262→    lw r0, 0(fp)

   264→    sw r0, 0(fp)

   266→    lw r2, 3(fp)
   267→    pop r0
   268→    sw r0, 0(r2)
   269→    add r2, 3
   270→    sw r2, 3(fp)
   271→    la r0, vm_loop
   272→    jmp (r0)
   273→
   274→; 0x03 — dup: duplicate top of eval stack
   275→op_dup:
   276→    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
   277→    lw r2, 3(fp)
   278→    ; r2 = esp
   279→    lw r0, -3(r2)
   280→    ; r0 = TOS value
   281→    sw r0, 0(r2)
   282→    add r2, 3
   283→    sw r2, 3(fp)
   284→    la r0, vm_loop
   285→    jmp (r0)
   286→
   287→; 0x04 — drop: discard top of eval stack
   288→op_drop:
   289→    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
   290→    lw r2, 3(fp)
   291→    add r2, -3
   292→    sw r2, 3(fp)
   293→    la r0, vm_loop
   294→    jmp (r0)
   295→
   296→; 0x05 — swap: ( a b -- b a )
   297→op_swap:
   298→    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
   299→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   300→    ; r0 = esp
   301→    add r0, -3
   302→    ; r0 = &b (TOS)
   303→    lw r2, 0(r0)
   304→    ; r2 = b
   305→    push r2
   306→    add r0, -3
   307→    ; r0 = &a (NOS)
   308→    lw r2, 0(r0)
   309→    ; r2 = a, hw stack top = b
   310→    ; Store a where b was
   311→    sw r2, 3(r0)
   312→    ; Store b where a was
   313→    pop r2
   314→    sw r2, 0(r0)
   315→    la r0, vm_loop
   316→    jmp (r0)
   317→
   318→; 0x06 — over: ( a b -- a b a )
   319→op_over:
   320→    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
   321→    lw r2, 3(fp)
   322→    ; r2 = esp
   323→    lw r0, -6(r2)
   324→    ; r0 = a (NOS)
   325→    sw r0, 0(r2)
   326→    add r2, 3
   327→    sw r2, 3(fp)
   328→    la r0, vm_loop
   329→    jmp (r0)
   330→
   331→; ============================================================
   332→; Arithmetic / Logic opcode handlers (0x10-0x1B)
   333→; ============================================================
   334→
   335→; 0x10 — add: ( a b -- a+b )
   336→op_add:
   337→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   338→    lw r1, -6(r0)
   339→    lw r2, -3(r0)
   340→    add r1, r2
   341→    sw r1, -6(r0)
   342→    add r0, -3
   343→    sw r0, 3(fp)
   344→    la r0, vm_loop
   345→    jmp (r0)
   346→
   347→; 0x11 — sub: ( a b -- a-b )
   348→op_sub:
   349→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   350→    lw r1, -6(r0)
   351→    lw r2, -3(r0)
   352→    sub r1, r2
   353→    sw r1, -6(r0)
   354→    add r0, -3
   355→    sw r0, 3(fp)
   356→    la r0, vm_loop
   357→    jmp (r0)
   358→
   359→; 0x12 — mul: ( a b -- a*b )
   360→op_mul:
   361→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   362→    lw r1, -6(r0)
   363→    lw r2, -3(r0)
   364→    mul r1, r2
   365→    sw r1, -6(r0)
   366→    add r0, -3
   367→    sw r0, 3(fp)
   368→    la r0, vm_loop
   369→    jmp (r0)
   370→
   371→; 0x13 — div: ( a b -- a/b ) signed, traps on b=0
   372→op_div:
   373→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   374→    lw r1, -6(r0)
   375→    lw r2, -3(r0)
   376→    push r0
   377→    ceq r2, z
   378→    brf div_ok
   379→    pop r0
   380→    lc r0, 2
   381→    sw r0, 21(fp)
   382→    lc r0, 1
   383→    sw r0, 24(fp)
   384→    la r0, vm_loop
   385→    jmp (r0)
   386→div_ok:
   387→    ; Compute sign of result: xor sign bits of a and b
   388→    mov r0, r1
   389→    xor r0, r2
   390→    push r0
   391→    ; Take |a|
   392→    cls r1, z
   393→    brf div_abs_a
   394→    lc r0, 0
   395→    sub r0, r1
   396→    mov r1, r0
   397→div_abs_a:
   398→    ; Take |b|
   399→    cls r2, z
   400→    brf div_abs_b
   401→    push r1
   402→    lc r1, 0
   403→    sub r1, r2
   404→    mov r2, r1
   405→    pop r1
   406→div_abs_b:
   407→    ; Unsigned divide: |a| / |b| by repeated subtraction
   408→    lc r0, 0
   409→div_loop:
   410→    clu r1, r2
   411→    brt div_done
   412→    sub r1, r2
   413→    add r0, 1
   414→    bra div_loop
   415→div_done:
   416→    ; r0 = quotient
   417→    mov r1, r0
   418→    ; Apply sign: negate if sign indicator < 0
   419→    pop r0
   420→    cls r0, z
   421→    brf div_pos
   422→    lc r0, 0
   423→    sub r0, r1
   424→    mov r1, r0
   425→div_pos:
   426→    pop r0
   427→    sw r1, -6(r0)
   428→    add r0, -3
   429→    sw r0, 3(fp)
   430→    la r0, vm_loop
   431→    jmp (r0)
   432→
   433→; 0x14 — mod: ( a b -- a%b ) signed, remainder sign = dividend sign
   434→op_mod:
   435→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   436→    lw r1, -6(r0)
   437→    lw r2, -3(r0)
   438→    push r0
   439→    ceq r2, z
   440→    brf mod_ok
   441→    pop r0
   442→    lc r0, 2
   443→    sw r0, 21(fp)
   444→    lc r0, 1
   445→    sw r0, 24(fp)
   446→    la r0, vm_loop
   447→    jmp (r0)
   448→mod_ok:
   449→    ; Save sign of dividend (remainder sign = dividend sign)
   450→    cls r1, z
   451→    brf mod_sign_pos
   452→    lc r0, 1
   453→    push r0
   454→    bra mod_sign_set
   455→mod_sign_pos:
   456→    lc r0, 0
   457→    push r0
   458→mod_sign_set:
   459→    ; Take |a|
   460→    cls r1, z
   461→    brf mod_abs_a
   462→    lc r0, 0
   463→    sub r0, r1
   464→    mov r1, r0
   465→mod_abs_a:
   466→    ; Take |b|
   467→    cls r2, z
   468→    brf mod_abs_b
   469→    push r1
   470→    lc r1, 0
   471→    sub r1, r2
   472→    mov r2, r1
   473→    pop r1
   474→mod_abs_b:
   475→    ; Unsigned mod: |a| % |b|
   476→mod_loop:
   477→    clu r1, r2
   478→    brt mod_done
   479→    sub r1, r2
   480→    bra mod_loop
   481→mod_done:
   482→    ; r1 = |remainder|
   483→    pop r0
   484→    ceq r0, z
   485→    brt mod_pos
   486→    lc r0, 0
   487→    sub r0, r1
   488→    mov r1, r0
   489→mod_pos:
   490→    pop r0
   491→    sw r1, -6(r0)
   492→    add r0, -3
   493→    sw r0, 3(fp)
   494→    la r0, vm_loop
   495→    jmp (r0)
   496→
   497→; 0x15 — neg: ( a -- -a )
   498→op_neg:
   499→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   500→    lw r1, -3(r0)
   501→    lc r0, 0
   502→    sub r0, r1
   503→    lw r2, 3(fp)
   504→    sw r0, -3(r2)
   505→    la r0, vm_loop
   506→    jmp (r0)
   507→
   508→; 0x16 — and: ( a b -- a&b )
   509→op_and:
   510→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   511→    lw r1, -6(r0)
   512→    lw r2, -3(r0)
   513→    and r1, r2
   514→    sw r1, -6(r0)
   515→    add r0, -3
   516→    sw r0, 3(fp)
   517→    la r0, vm_loop
   518→    jmp (r0)
   519→
   520→; 0x17 — or: ( a b -- a|b )
   521→op_or:
   522→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   523→    lw r1, -6(r0)
   524→    lw r2, -3(r0)
   525→    or r1, r2
   526→    sw r1, -6(r0)
   527→    add r0, -3
   528→    sw r0, 3(fp)
   529→    la r0, vm_loop
   530→    jmp (r0)
   531→
   532→; 0x18 — xor: ( a b -- a^b )
   533→op_xor:
   534→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   535→    lw r1, -6(r0)
   536→    lw r2, -3(r0)
   537→    xor r1, r2
   538→    sw r1, -6(r0)
   539→    add r0, -3
   540→    sw r0, 3(fp)
   541→    la r0, vm_loop
   542→    jmp (r0)
   543→
   544→; 0x19 — not: ( a -- ~a ) bitwise complement via xor with -1
   545→op_not:
   546→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   547→    lw r1, -3(r0)
   548→    la r0, -1
   549→    xor r0, r1
   550→    lw r2, 3(fp)
   551→    sw r0, -3(r2)
   552→    la r0, vm_loop
   553→    jmp (r0)
   554→
   555→; 0x1A — shl: ( a n -- a<<n )
   556→op_shl:
   557→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   558→    lw r1, -6(r0)
   559→    lw r2, -3(r0)
   560→    shl r1, r2
   561→    sw r1, -6(r0)
   562→    add r0, -3
   563→    sw r0, 3(fp)
   564→    la r0, vm_loop
   565→    jmp (r0)
   566→
   567→; 0x1B — shr: ( a n -- a>>n ) arithmetic shift right
   568→op_shr:
   569→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   570→    lw r1, -6(r0)
   571→    lw r2, -3(r0)
   572→    sra r1, r2
   573→    sw r1, -6(r0)
   574→    add r0, -3
   575→    sw r0, 3(fp)
   576→    la r0, vm_loop
   577→    jmp (r0)
   578→
   579→; ============================================================
   580→; Comparison opcode handlers (0x20-0x25)
   581→; All pop two values, push 1 (true) or 0 (false)
   582→; ============================================================
   583→
   584→; 0x20 — eq: ( a b -- flag ) a == b
   585→op_eq:
   586→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   587→    lw r1, -6(r0)
   588→    lw r2, -3(r0)
   589→    ceq r1, r2
   590→    brt eq_true
   591→    lc r1, 0
   592→    bra eq_done
   593→eq_true:
   594→    lc r1, 1
   595→eq_done:
   596→    sw r1, -6(r0)
   597→    add r0, -3
   598→    sw r0, 3(fp)
   599→    la r0, vm_loop
   600→    jmp (r0)
   601→
   602→; 0x21 — ne: ( a b -- flag ) a != b
   603→op_ne:
   604→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   605→    lw r1, -6(r0)
   606→    lw r2, -3(r0)
   607→    ceq r1, r2
   608→    brf ne_true
   609→    lc r1, 0
   610→    bra ne_done
   611→ne_true:
   612→    lc r1, 1
   613→ne_done:
   614→    sw r1, -6(r0)
   615→    add r0, -3
   616→    sw r0, 3(fp)
   617→    la r0, vm_loop
   618→    jmp (r0)
   619→
   620→; 0x22 — lt: ( a b -- flag ) a < b (signed)
   621→op_lt:
   622→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   623→    lw r1, -6(r0)
   624→    lw r2, -3(r0)
   625→    cls r1, r2
   626→    brt lt_true
   627→    lc r1, 0
   628→    bra lt_done
   629→lt_true:
   630→    lc r1, 1
   631→lt_done:
   632→    sw r1, -6(r0)
   633→    add r0, -3
   634→    sw r0, 3(fp)
   635→    la r0, vm_loop
   636→    jmp (r0)
   637→
   638→; 0x23 — le: ( a b -- flag ) a <= b (signed) = !(b < a)
   639→op_le:
   640→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   641→    lw r1, -6(r0)
   642→    lw r2, -3(r0)
   643→    cls r2, r1
   644→    brf le_true
   645→    lc r1, 0
   646→    bra le_done
   647→le_true:
   648→    lc r1, 1
   649→le_done:
   650→    sw r1, -6(r0)
   651→    add r0, -3
   652→    sw r0, 3(fp)
   653→    la r0, vm_loop
   654→    jmp (r0)
   655→
   656→; 0x24 — gt: ( a b -- flag ) a > b (signed) = b < a
   657→op_gt:
   658→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   659→    lw r1, -6(r0)
   660→    lw r2, -3(r0)
   661→    cls r2, r1
   662→    brt gt_true
   663→    lc r1, 0
   664→    bra gt_done
   665→gt_true:
   666→    lc r1, 1
   667→gt_done:
   668→    sw r1, -6(r0)
   669→    add r0, -3
   670→    sw r0, 3(fp)
   671→    la r0, vm_loop
   672→    jmp (r0)
   673→
   674→; 0x25 — ge: ( a b -- flag ) a >= b (signed) = !(a < b)
   675→op_ge:
   676→    lw r0, 3(fp)
   677→    lw r1, -6(r0)
   678→    lw r2, -3(r0)
   679→    cls r1, r2
   680→    brf ge_true
   681→    lc r1, 0
   682→    bra ge_done
   683→ge_true:
   684→    lc r1, 1
   685→ge_done:
   686→    sw r1, -6(r0)
   687→    add r0, -3
   688→    sw r0, 3(fp)
   689→    la r0, vm_loop
   690→    jmp (r0)
   691→
   692→; 0x60 — sys id8: system call dispatch
   693→op_sys:
   694→    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
   695→    ; Fetch sys ID byte
   696→    lw r0, 18(fp)
   697→    lw r2, 0(fp)
   698→    add r0, r2
   699→    lbu r2, 0(r0)
   700→    ; r2 = sys id
   701→    ; Increment pc
   702→    lw r0, 0(fp)
   703→    add r0, 1
   704→    sw r0, 0(fp)
   705→    ; Dispatch on sys id
   706→    mov r0, r2
   707→    lc r2, 1
   708→    ceq r0, r2
   709→    brt sys_putc
   710→    ; Unknown sys id — trap
   711→    la r0, op_invalid
   712→    jmp (r0)
   713→
   714→; sys PUTC (id=1): pop char from eval stack, send to UART
   715→sys_putc:
   716→    ; fp = &vm_state
   717→    lw r2, 3(fp)
   718→    add r2, -3
   719→    sw r2, 3(fp)
   720→    ; r2 = new esp = &TOS
   721→    lw r0, 0(r2)
   722→    ; r0 = char value
   723→    push r0
   724→    la r2, -65280
   725→sys_putc_wait:
   726→    lb r0, 1(r2)
   727→    cls r0, z
   728→    brt sys_putc_wait
   729→    pop r0
   730→    sb r0, 0(r2)
   731→    la r0, vm_loop
   732→    jmp (r0)
   733→
   734→; Invalid opcode: set status=2, trap_code=4 (INVALID_OPCODE)
   735→op_invalid:
   736→    la r0, vm_state
   737→    push r0
   738→    pop fp
   739→    lc r0, 2
   740→    sw r0, 21(fp)
   741→    lc r0, 4
   742→    sw r0, 24(fp)
   743→    la r0, vm_loop
   744→    jmp (r0)
   745→
   746→; Stub handler — all unimplemented opcodes trap as invalid
   747→op_stub:
   748→    la r0, op_invalid
   749→    jmp (r0)

; pvm.s — P-Code Virtual Machine for COR24
;
; Register allocation:
;   r0 = W (work/scratch, opcode dispatch)
;   r1 = scratch / return address for jal calls
;   r2 = scratch
;   sp = COR24 hardware stack (EBR, used sparingly)
;   fp = memory base for indexed loads/stores
;
; VM state lives in memory struct (vm_state) because COR24 has only 3 GPRs.
;
; UART: data at -65280 (0xFF0100), status at -65279 (0xFF0101)
;   TX busy = status bit 7 (sign bit via lb sign-extend)
;   RX ready = status bit 0
; LED: port at -65024 (0xFF0200)
;
; COR24 ISA notes:
;   lbu = load byte zero-extend, lb = load byte sign-extend
;   ceq ra, rb sets C if ra == rb; cls ra, rb sets C if ra < rb (signed)
;   clu ra, rb sets C if ra < rb (unsigned)
;   brt/brf = branch if C true/false; bra = branch always
;   jal r1, (r2) = r1 = PC+1, PC = r2 (call convention)
;   jmp (r1) = return from jal call
;   Valid load/store base registers: r0, r1, r2, fp (NOT sp)
;   Can push/pop: r0, r1, r2, fp

; ============================================================
; Entry point
; ============================================================
_start:
    ; Initialize VM state struct (fp = &vm_state)
    la r0, vm_state
    push r0
    pop fp

    ; pc = 0
    lc r0, 0
    sw r0, 0(fp)

    ; esp = eval_stack base
    la r0, eval_stack
    sw r0, 3(fp)

    ; csp = call_stack base
    la r0, call_stack
    sw r0, 6(fp)

    ; fp_vm = 0 (no frame yet)
    lc r0, 0
    sw r0, 9(fp)

    ; gp = globals base
    la r0, globals_seg
    sw r0, 12(fp)

    ; hp = heap base
    la r0, heap_seg
    sw r0, 15(fp)

    ; code = code segment base
    la r0, code_seg
    sw r0, 18(fp)

    ; status = 0 (running)
    lc r0, 0
    sw r0, 21(fp)

    ; trap_code = 0
    lc r0, 0
    sw r0, 24(fp)

    ; Print boot message
    la r0, msg_boot
    la r2, uart_puts
    jal r1, (r2)

    ; Enter VM main loop
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; ============================================================
; UART helpers
; ============================================================

; uart_putc — send byte in r0 to UART
; Call via: jal r1, (r2) with r2 = uart_putc
; Clobbers: r0, r2 (r1 = return address, preserved)
uart_putc:
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
uart_putc_wait:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt uart_putc_wait
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    jmp (r1)

; uart_puts — print null-terminated string at address in r0
; Call via: jal r1, (r2) with r2 = uart_puts, r0 = string addr
; Clobbers: r0, r1, r2
uart_puts:
    push r1
    mov r1, r0
uart_puts_loop:
    lbu r0, 0(r1)
    ceq r0, z
    brt uart_puts_done
    push r1
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
uart_puts_tx:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt uart_puts_tx
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    pop r1
    add r1, 1
    bra uart_puts_loop
uart_puts_done:
    pop r1
    jmp (r1)

; ============================================================
; VM fetch-decode-execute loop
; ============================================================
vm_loop:
    ; Check status: if not running (0), stop
    la r0, vm_state
    push r0
    pop fp
    lw r0, 21(fp)
    ceq r0, z
    brf vm_halted

    ; Fetch opcode byte from code[pc]
    lw r0, 18(fp)
    lw r2, 0(fp)
    add r0, r2
    ; r0 = code_base + pc, use r0 as base register
    lbu r2, 0(r0)
    ; r2 = opcode byte (zero-extended)

    ; Increment pc
    lw r0, 0(fp)
    add r0, 1
    sw r0, 0(fp)

    ; Bounds check: opcode must be < 97 (0x00..0x60)
    mov r0, r2
    lc r2, 97
    clu r0, r2
    brt opcode_ok
    la r0, op_invalid
    jmp (r0)
opcode_ok:

    ; Dispatch: compute dispatch_table[opcode * 3]
    mov r2, r0
    add r0, r0
    add r0, r2
    ; r0 = 3 * opcode
    la r2, dispatch_table
    add r2, r0
    ; r2 = &dispatch_table[opcode * 3]
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    ; r0 = handler address
    jmp (r0)

; ============================================================
; VM halt / trap exit
; ============================================================
vm_halted:
    ; r0 = status (nonzero)
    ; Check if trapped (status == 2)
    lc r2, 2
    ceq r0, r2
    brt vm_trapped
    ; Normal halt
    la r0, msg_halted
    la r2, uart_puts_final
    jal r1, (r2)
vm_trapped:
    la r0, msg_trap
    la r2, uart_puts_final
    jal r1, (r2)

; uart_puts_final — print string then enter halt loop
; r0 = string address. Does not return.
uart_puts_final:
    mov r1, r0
uart_puts_final_loop:
    lbu r0, 0(r1)
    ceq r0, z
    brt halt_loop
    push r1
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
uart_puts_final_tx:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt uart_puts_final_tx
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    pop r1
    add r1, 1
    bra uart_puts_final_loop

halt_loop:
    bra halt_loop

; ============================================================
; Opcode handlers (all stubs for now)
; ============================================================

; 0x00 — halt: set status=1, return to vm_loop
op_halt:
    la r0, vm_state
    push r0
    pop fp
    lc r0, 1
    sw r0, 21(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x01 — push imm24: fetch 3-byte operand, push onto eval stack
op_push:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    lw r0, 18(fp)
    lw r2, 0(fp)
    add r0, r2
    ; r0 = &code[pc]
    lw r2, 0(r0)
    ; r2 = 24-bit immediate
    push r2
    ; Increment pc by 3
    lw r0, 0(fp)
    add r0, 3
    sw r0, 0(fp)
    ; Push r2 onto eval stack
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    ; r2 = esp
    pop r0
    sw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x02 — push_s imm8: fetch 1-byte sign-extended operand, push onto eval stack
op_push_s:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    lw r0, 18(fp)
    lw r2, 0(fp)
    add r0, r2
    ; r0 = &code[pc]
    lb r2, 0(r0)
    ; r2 = sign-extended byte operand
    push r2
    ; Increment pc by 1
    lw r0, 0(fp)
    add r0, 1
    sw r0, 0(fp)
    ; Push r2 onto eval stack
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    pop r0
    sw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x03 — dup: duplicate top of eval stack
op_dup:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    ; r2 = esp
    lw r0, -3(r2)
    ; r0 = TOS value
    sw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x04 — drop: discard top of eval stack
op_drop:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    add r2, -3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x05 — swap: ( a b -- b a )
op_swap:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    ; r0 = esp
    add r0, -3
    ; r0 = &b (TOS)
    lw r2, 0(r0)
    ; r2 = b
    push r2
    add r0, -3
    ; r0 = &a (NOS)
    lw r2, 0(r0)
    ; r2 = a, hw stack top = b
    ; Store a where b was
    sw r2, 3(r0)
    ; Store b where a was
    pop r2
    sw r2, 0(r0)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x06 — over: ( a b -- a b a )
op_over:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    ; r2 = esp
    lw r0, -6(r2)
    ; r0 = a (NOS)
    sw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; ============================================================
; Arithmetic / Logic opcode handlers (0x10-0x1B)
; ============================================================

; 0x10 — add: ( a b -- a+b )
op_add:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    add r1, r2
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x11 — sub: ( a b -- a-b )
op_sub:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    sub r1, r2
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x12 — mul: ( a b -- a*b )
op_mul:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    mul r1, r2
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x13 — div: ( a b -- a/b ) signed, traps on b=0
op_div:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    push r0
    ceq r2, z
    brf div_ok
    pop r0
    lc r0, 2
    sw r0, 21(fp)
    lc r0, 1
    sw r0, 24(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)
div_ok:
    ; Compute sign of result: xor sign bits of a and b
    mov r0, r1
    xor r0, r2
    push r0
    ; Take |a|
    cls r1, z
    brf div_abs_a
    lc r0, 0
    sub r0, r1
    mov r1, r0
div_abs_a:
    ; Take |b|
    cls r2, z
    brf div_abs_b
    push r1
    lc r1, 0
    sub r1, r2
    mov r2, r1
    pop r1
div_abs_b:
    ; Unsigned divide: |a| / |b| by repeated subtraction
    lc r0, 0
div_loop:
    clu r1, r2
    brt div_done
    sub r1, r2
    add r0, 1
    bra div_loop
div_done:
    ; r0 = quotient
    mov r1, r0
    ; Apply sign: negate if sign indicator < 0
    pop r0
    cls r0, z
    brf div_pos
    lc r0, 0
    sub r0, r1
    mov r1, r0
div_pos:
    pop r0
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x14 — mod: ( a b -- a%b ) signed, remainder sign = dividend sign
op_mod:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    push r0
    ceq r2, z
    brf mod_ok
    pop r0
    lc r0, 2
    sw r0, 21(fp)
    lc r0, 1
    sw r0, 24(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)
mod_ok:
    ; Save sign of dividend (remainder sign = dividend sign)
    cls r1, z
    brf mod_sign_pos
    lc r0, 1
    push r0
    bra mod_sign_set
mod_sign_pos:
    lc r0, 0
    push r0
mod_sign_set:
    ; Take |a|
    cls r1, z
    brf mod_abs_a
    lc r0, 0
    sub r0, r1
    mov r1, r0
mod_abs_a:
    ; Take |b|
    cls r2, z
    brf mod_abs_b
    push r1
    lc r1, 0
    sub r1, r2
    mov r2, r1
    pop r1
mod_abs_b:
    ; Unsigned mod: |a| % |b|
mod_loop:
    clu r1, r2
    brt mod_done
    sub r1, r2
    bra mod_loop
mod_done:
    ; r1 = |remainder|
    pop r0
    ceq r0, z
    brt mod_pos
    lc r0, 0
    sub r0, r1
    mov r1, r0
mod_pos:
    pop r0
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x15 — neg: ( a -- -a )
op_neg:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -3(r0)
    lc r0, 0
    sub r0, r1
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    sw r0, -3(r2)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x16 — and: ( a b -- a&b )
op_and:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    and r1, r2
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x17 — or: ( a b -- a|b )
op_or:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    or r1, r2
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x18 — xor: ( a b -- a^b )
op_xor:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    xor r1, r2
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x19 — not: ( a -- ~a ) bitwise complement via xor with -1
op_not:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -3(r0)
    la r0, -1
    xor r0, r1
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    sw r0, -3(r2)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x1A — shl: ( a n -- a<<n )
op_shl:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    shl r1, r2
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x1B — shr: ( a n -- a>>n ) arithmetic shift right
op_shr:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    sra r1, r2
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; ============================================================
; Comparison opcode handlers (0x20-0x25)
; All pop two values, push 1 (true) or 0 (false)
; ============================================================

; 0x20 — eq: ( a b -- flag ) a == b
op_eq:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    ceq r1, r2
    brt eq_true
    lc r1, 0
    bra eq_done
eq_true:
    lc r1, 1
eq_done:
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x21 — ne: ( a b -- flag ) a != b
op_ne:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    ceq r1, r2
    brf ne_true
    lc r1, 0
    bra ne_done
ne_true:
    lc r1, 1
ne_done:
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x22 — lt: ( a b -- flag ) a < b (signed)
op_lt:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    cls r1, r2
    brt lt_true
    lc r1, 0
    bra lt_done
lt_true:
    lc r1, 1
lt_done:
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x23 — le: ( a b -- flag ) a <= b (signed) = !(b < a)
op_le:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    cls r2, r1
    brf le_true
    lc r1, 0
    bra le_done
le_true:
    lc r1, 1
le_done:
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x24 — gt: ( a b -- flag ) a > b (signed) = b < a
op_gt:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    cls r2, r1
    brt gt_true
    lc r1, 0
    bra gt_done
gt_true:
    lc r1, 1
gt_done:
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x25 — ge: ( a b -- flag ) a >= b (signed) = !(a < b)
op_ge:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    cls r1, r2
    brf ge_true
    lc r1, 0
    bra ge_done
ge_true:
    lc r1, 1
ge_done:
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x60 — sys id8: system call dispatch
op_sys:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    ; Fetch sys ID byte
    lw r0, 18(fp)
    lw r2, 0(fp)
    add r0, r2
    lbu r2, 0(r0)
    ; r2 = sys id
    ; Increment pc
    lw r0, 0(fp)
    add r0, 1
    sw r0, 0(fp)
    ; Dispatch on sys id
    mov r0, r2
    lc r2, 1
    ceq r0, r2
    brt sys_putc
    ; Unknown sys id — trap
    la r0, op_invalid
    jmp (r0)

; sys PUTC (id=1): pop char from eval stack, send to UART
sys_putc:
    ; fp = &vm_state
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    add r2, -3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    ; r2 = new esp = &TOS
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    ; r0 = char value
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
sys_putc_wait:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt sys_putc_wait
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; Invalid opcode: set status=2, trap_code=4 (INVALID_OPCODE)
op_invalid:
    la r0, vm_state
    push r0
    pop fp
    lc r0, 2
    sw r0, 21(fp)
    lc r0, 4
    sw r0, 24(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; Stub handler — all unimplemented opcodes trap as invalid
op_stub:
    la r0, op_invalid
    jmp (r0)

; ============================================================
; Dispatch table (97 entries: opcodes 0x00 through 0x60)
; Each entry is a .word (3 bytes) holding the handler address
; ============================================================
dispatch_table:
    ; 0x00-0x06: Stack / Constants
    .word op_halt
    .word op_push
    .word op_push_s
    .word op_dup
    .word op_drop
    .word op_swap
    .word op_over
    ; 0x07-0x0F: reserved (gap)
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid

toolUseResult.originalFile:
; pvm.s — P-Code Virtual Machine for COR24
;
; Register allocation:
;   r0 = W (work/scratch, opcode dispatch)
;   r1 = scratch / return address for jal calls
;   r2 = scratch
;   sp = COR24 hardware stack (EBR, used sparingly)
;   fp = memory base for indexed loads/stores
;
; VM state lives in memory struct (vm_state) because COR24 has only 3 GPRs.
;
; UART: data at -65280 (0xFF0100), status at -65279 (0xFF0101)
;   TX busy = status bit 7 (sign bit via lb sign-extend)
;   RX ready = status bit 0
; LED: port at -65024 (0xFF0200)
;
; COR24 ISA notes:
;   lbu = load byte zero-extend, lb = load byte sign-extend
;   ceq ra, rb sets C if ra == rb; cls ra, rb sets C if ra < rb (signed)
;   clu ra, rb sets C if ra < rb (unsigned)
;   brt/brf = branch if C true/false; bra = branch always
;   jal r1, (r2) = r1 = PC+1, PC = r2 (call convention)
;   jmp (r1) = return from jal call
;   Valid load/store base registers: r0, r1, r2, fp (NOT sp)
;   Can push/pop: r0, r1, r2, fp

; ============================================================
; Entry point
; ============================================================
_start:
    ; Initialize VM state struct (fp = &vm_state)
    la r0, vm_state
    push r0
    pop fp

    ; pc = 0
    lc r0, 0
    sw r0, 0(fp)

    ; esp = eval_stack base
    la r0, eval_stack
    sw r0, 3(fp)

    ; csp = call_stack base
    la r0, call_stack
    sw r0, 6(fp)

    ; fp_vm = 0 (no frame yet)
    lc r0, 0
    sw r0, 9(fp)

    ; gp = globals base
    la r0, globals_seg
    sw r0, 12(fp)

    ; hp = heap base
    la r0, heap_seg
    sw r0, 15(fp)

    ; code = code segment base
    la r0, code_seg
    sw r0, 18(fp)

    ; status = 0 (running)
    lc r0, 0
    sw r0, 21(fp)

    ; trap_code = 0
    lc r0, 0
    sw r0, 24(fp)

    ; Print boot message
    la r0, msg_boot
    la r2, uart_puts
    jal r1, (r2)

    ; Enter VM main loop
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; ============================================================
; UART helpers
; ============================================================

; uart_putc — send byte in r0 to UART
; Call via: jal r1, (r2) with r2 = uart_putc
; Clobbers: r0, r2 (r1 = return address, preserved)
uart_putc:
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
uart_putc_wait:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt uart_putc_wait
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    jmp (r1)

; uart_puts — print null-terminated string at address in r0
; Call via: jal r1, (r2) with r2 = uart_puts, r0 = string addr
; Clobbers: r0, r1, r2
uart_puts:
    push r1
    mov r1, r0
uart_puts_loop:
    lbu r0, 0(r1)
    ceq r0, z
    brt uart_puts_done
    push r1
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
uart_puts_tx:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt uart_puts_tx
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    pop r1
    add r1, 1
    bra uart_puts_loop
uart_puts_done:
    pop r1
    jmp (r1)

; ============================================================
; VM fetch-decode-execute loop
; ============================================================
vm_loop:
    ; Check status: if not running (0), stop
    la r0, vm_state
    push r0
    pop fp
    lw r0, 21(fp)
    ceq r0, z
    brf vm_halted

    ; Fetch opcode byte from code[pc]
    lw r0, 18(fp)
    lw r2, 0(fp)
    add r0, r2
    ; r0 = code_base + pc, use r0 as base register
    lbu r2, 0(r0)
    ; r2 = opcode byte (zero-extended)

    ; Increment pc
    lw r0, 0(fp)
    add r0, 1
    sw r0, 0(fp)

    ; Bounds check: opcode must be < 97 (0x00..0x60)
    mov r0, r2
    lc r2, 97
    clu r0, r2
    brt opcode_ok
    la r0, op_invalid
    jmp (r0)
opcode_ok:

    ; Dispatch: compute dispatch_table[opcode * 3]
    mov r2, r0
    add r0, r0
    add r0, r2
    ; r0 = 3 * opcode
    la r2, dispatch_table
    add r2, r0
    ; r2 = &dispatch_table[opcode * 3]
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    ; r0 = handler address
    jmp (r0)

; ============================================================
; VM halt / trap exit
; ============================================================
vm_halted:
    ; r0 = status (nonzero)
    ; Check if trapped (status == 2)
    lc r2, 2
    ceq r0, r2
    brt vm_trapped
    ; Normal halt
    la r0, msg_halted
    la r2, uart_puts_final
    jal r1, (r2)
vm_trapped:
    la r0, msg_trap
    la r2, uart_puts_final
    jal r1, (r2)

; uart_puts_final — print string then enter halt loop
; r0 = string address. Does not return.
uart_puts_final:
    mov r1, r0
uart_puts_final_loop:
    lbu r0, 0(r1)
    ceq r0, z
    brt halt_loop
    push r1
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
uart_puts_final_tx:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt uart_puts_final_tx
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    pop r1
    add r1, 1
    bra uart_puts_final_loop

halt_loop:
    bra halt_loop

; ============================================================
; Opcode handlers (all stubs for now)
; ============================================================

; 0x00 — halt: set status=1, return to vm_loop
op_halt:
    la r0, vm_state
    push r0
    pop fp
    lc r0, 1
    sw r0, 21(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x01 — push imm24: fetch 3-byte operand, push onto eval stack
op_push:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    lw r0, 18(fp)
    lw r2, 0(fp)
    add r0, r2
    ; r0 = &code[pc]
    lw r2, 0(r0)
    ; r2 = 24-bit immediate
    push r2
    ; Increment pc by 3
    lw r0, 0(fp)
    add r0, 3
    sw r0, 0(fp)
    ; Push r2 onto eval stack
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    ; r2 = esp
    pop r0
    sw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x02 — push_s imm8: fetch 1-byte sign-extended operand, push onto eval stack
op_push_s:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    lw r0, 18(fp)
    lw r2, 0(fp)
    add r0, r2
    ; r0 = &code[pc]
    lb r2, 0(r0)
    ; r2 = sign-extended byte operand
    push r2
    ; Increment pc by 1
    lw r0, 0(fp)
    add r0, 1
    sw r0, 0(fp)
    ; Push r2 onto eval stack
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    pop r0
    sw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x03 — dup: duplicate top of eval stack
op_dup:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    ; r2 = esp
    lw r0, -3(r2)
    ; r0 = TOS value
    sw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x04 — drop: discard top of eval stack
op_drop:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    add r2, -3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x05 — swap: ( a b -- b a )
op_swap:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    ; r0 = esp
    add r0, -3
    ; r0 = &b (TOS)
    lw r2, 0(r0)
    ; r2 = b
    push r2
    add r0, -3
    ; r0 = &a (NOS)
    lw r2, 0(r0)
    ; r2 = a, hw stack top = b
    ; Store a where b was
    sw r2, 3(r0)
    ; Store b where a was
    pop r2
    sw r2, 0(r0)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x06 — over: ( a b -- a b a )
op_over:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    ; r2 = esp
    lw r0, -6(r2)
    ; r0 = a (NOS)
    sw r0, 0(r2)
    add r2, 3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; ============================================================
; Arithmetic / Logic opcode handlers (0x10-0x1B)
; ============================================================

; 0x10 — add: ( a b -- a+b )
op_add:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    add r1, r2
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x11 — sub: ( a b -- a-b )
op_sub:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    sub r1, r2
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x12 — mul: ( a b -- a*b )
op_mul:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    mul r1, r2
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x13 — div: ( a b -- a/b ) signed, traps on b=0
op_div:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    push r0
    ceq r2, z
    brf div_ok
    pop r0
    lc r0, 2
    sw r0, 21(fp)
    lc r0, 1
    sw r0, 24(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)
div_ok:
    ; Compute sign of result: xor sign bits of a and b
    mov r0, r1
    xor r0, r2
    push r0
    ; Take |a|
    cls r1, z
    brf div_abs_a
    lc r0, 0
    sub r0, r1
    mov r1, r0
div_abs_a:
    ; Take |b|
    cls r2, z
    brf div_abs_b
    push r1
    lc r1, 0
    sub r1, r2
    mov r2, r1
    pop r1
div_abs_b:
    ; Unsigned divide: |a| / |b| by repeated subtraction
    lc r0, 0
div_loop:
    clu r1, r2
    brt div_done
    sub r1, r2
    add r0, 1
    bra div_loop
div_done:
    ; r0 = quotient
    mov r1, r0
    ; Apply sign: negate if sign indicator < 0
    pop r0
    cls r0, z
    brf div_pos
    lc r0, 0
    sub r0, r1
    mov r1, r0
div_pos:
    pop r0
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x14 — mod: ( a b -- a%b ) signed, remainder sign = dividend sign
op_mod:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    push r0
    ceq r2, z
    brf mod_ok
    pop r0
    lc r0, 2
    sw r0, 21(fp)
    lc r0, 1
    sw r0, 24(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)
mod_ok:
    ; Save sign of dividend (remainder sign = dividend sign)
    cls r1, z
    brf mod_sign_pos
    lc r0, 1
    push r0
    bra mod_sign_set
mod_sign_pos:
    lc r0, 0
    push r0
mod_sign_set:
    ; Take |a|
    cls r1, z
    brf mod_abs_a
    lc r0, 0
    sub r0, r1
    mov r1, r0
mod_abs_a:
    ; Take |b|
    cls r2, z
    brf mod_abs_b
    push r1
    lc r1, 0
    sub r1, r2
    mov r2, r1
    pop r1
mod_abs_b:
    ; Unsigned mod: |a| % |b|
mod_loop:
    clu r1, r2
    brt mod_done
    sub r1, r2
    bra mod_loop
mod_done:
    ; r1 = |remainder|
    pop r0
    ceq r0, z
    brt mod_pos
    lc r0, 0
    sub r0, r1
    mov r1, r0
mod_pos:
    pop r0
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x15 — neg: ( a -- -a )
op_neg:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -3(r0)
    lc r0, 0
    sub r0, r1
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    sw r0, -3(r2)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x16 — and: ( a b -- a&b )
op_and:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    and r1, r2
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x17 — or: ( a b -- a|b )
op_or:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    or r1, r2
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x18 — xor: ( a b -- a^b )
op_xor:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    xor r1, r2
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x19 — not: ( a -- ~a ) bitwise complement via xor with -1
op_not:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -3(r0)
    la r0, -1
    xor r0, r1
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    sw r0, -3(r2)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x1A — shl: ( a n -- a<<n )
op_shl:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    shl r1, r2
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x1B — shr: ( a n -- a>>n ) arithmetic shift right
op_shr:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    sra r1, r2
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; ============================================================
; Comparison opcode handlers (0x20-0x25)
; All pop two values, push 1 (true) or 0 (false)
; ============================================================

; 0x20 — eq: ( a b -- flag ) a == b
op_eq:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    ceq r1, r2
    brt eq_true
    lc r1, 0
    bra eq_done
eq_true:
    lc r1, 1
eq_done:
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x21 — ne: ( a b -- flag ) a != b
op_ne:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    ceq r1, r2
    brf ne_true
    lc r1, 0
    bra ne_done
ne_true:
    lc r1, 1
ne_done:
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x22 — lt: ( a b -- flag ) a < b (signed)
op_lt:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    cls r1, r2
    brt lt_true
    lc r1, 0
    bra lt_done
lt_true:
    lc r1, 1
lt_done:
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x23 — le: ( a b -- flag ) a <= b (signed) = !(b < a)
op_le:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    cls r2, r1
    brf le_true
    lc r1, 0
    bra le_done
le_true:
    lc r1, 1
le_done:
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x24 — gt: ( a b -- flag ) a > b (signed) = b < a
op_gt:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    cls r2, r1
    brt gt_true
    lc r1, 0
    bra gt_done
gt_true:
    lc r1, 1
gt_done:
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x25 — ge: ( a b -- flag ) a >= b (signed) = !(a < b)
op_ge:
    lw r0, 3(fp)
    lw r1, -6(r0)
    lw r2, -3(r0)
    cls r1, r2
    brf ge_true
    lc r1, 0
    bra ge_done
ge_true:
    lc r1, 1
ge_done:
    sw r1, -6(r0)
    add r0, -3
    sw r0, 3(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; 0x60 — sys id8: system call dispatch
op_sys:
    ; fp = &vm_state from dispatch
    ; Fetch sys ID byte
    lw r0, 18(fp)
    lw r2, 0(fp)
    add r0, r2
    lbu r2, 0(r0)
    ; r2 = sys id
    ; Increment pc
    lw r0, 0(fp)
    add r0, 1
    sw r0, 0(fp)
    ; Dispatch on sys id
    mov r0, r2
    lc r2, 1
    ceq r0, r2
    brt sys_putc
    ; Unknown sys id — trap
    la r0, op_invalid
    jmp (r0)

; sys PUTC (id=1): pop char from eval stack, send to UART
sys_putc:
    ; fp = &vm_state
    lw r2, 3(fp)
    add r2, -3
    sw r2, 3(fp)
    ; r2 = new esp = &TOS
    lw r0, 0(r2)
    ; r0 = char value
    push r0
    la r2, -65280
sys_putc_wait:
    lb r0, 1(r2)
    cls r0, z
    brt sys_putc_wait
    pop r0
    sb r0, 0(r2)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; Invalid opcode: set status=2, trap_code=4 (INVALID_OPCODE)
op_invalid:
    la r0, vm_state
    push r0
    pop fp
    lc r0, 2
    sw r0, 21(fp)
    lc r0, 4
    sw r0, 24(fp)
    la r0, vm_loop
    jmp (r0)

; Stub handler — all unimplemented opcodes trap as invalid
op_stub:
    la r0, op_invalid
    jmp (r0)

; ============================================================
; Dispatch table (97 entries: opcodes 0x00 through 0x60)
; Each entry is a .word (3 bytes) holding the handler address
; ============================================================
dispatch_table:
    ; 0x00-0x06: Stack / Constants
    .word op_halt
    .word op_push
    .word op_push_s
    .word op_dup
    .word op_drop
    .word op_swap
    .word op_over
    ; 0x07-0x0F: reserved (gap)
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    ; 0x10-0x1B: Arithmetic / Logic
    .word op_add
    .word op_sub
    .word op_mul
    .word op_div
    .word op_mod
    .word op_neg
    .word op_and
    .word op_or
    .word op_xor
    .word op_not
    .word op_shl
    .word op_shr
    ; 0x1C-0x1F: reserved (gap)
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    ; 0x20-0x25: Comparison
    .word op_eq
    .word op_ne
    .word op_lt
    .word op_le
    .word op_gt
    .word op_ge
    ; 0x26-0x2F: reserved (gap)
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    ; 0x30-0x35: Control Flow
    .word op_stub
    .word op_stub

    .word op_stub
    .word op_stub
    .word op_stub
    ; 0x36-0x3F: reserved (gap)
    .word op_invalid

    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    .word op_invalid
    ; 0x40-0x4B: Local / Global / Nonlocal Access
    .word op_stub
    .word op_stub
    .word op_stub
    .word op_stub
    .word op_stub
    .word op_stub
    .word op_stub
    .word op_stub
    .word op_stub

    .word op_invalid

    ; 0x50-0x53: Indirect Memory Access
    .word op_stub

    .word op_invalid

; ============================================================
; String constants
; ============================================================
